task_completion.description: Create a 60x60mm square sketch centered on the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch of a square is visible in the SolveSpace workspace, with dimensions showing 60mm for the sides, and it appears centered on the origin axes.

task_completion.description: Extrude the square sketch to create a 10mm thick base plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The square sketch is extruded into a 3D rectangular prism. The Property Browser should show an extrusion depth of 10mm for this group.

task_completion.description: Create a 30mm diameter circular sketch centered on the top face of the base plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D circle is sketched on the top surface of the previously extruded base plate. A diameter dimension of 30mm (or radius of 15mm) should be visible, and it should be centered.

task_completion.description: Extrude the circular sketch upward to a height of 50mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D cylinder protrudes upward from the square base. The Property Browser should show an extrusion depth of 50mm for this new group.

task_completion.description: Save the completed model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar displays 'square_flange_shaft.slvs', or a save dialog is visible showing the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the SolveSpace graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application open and being actively used with the mouse/menus, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The part was built step-by-step rather than imported or loaded from a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace Property Browser (usually on the left) shows a history of multiple groups (e.g., Sketch in 3d, Extrude, Sketch in 3d, Extrude) indicating genuine construction.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass manual CAD modeling.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows visible where the agent runs scripts (e.g., Python) to generate the .slvs file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/square_flange_shaft.slvs. The final model must have at least 2 extrusion groups. The total height of the part should be 60mm (10mm base + 50mm shaft), and the base size should be 60x60mm. A tolerance of ±12mm is acceptable for the bounding box dimensions.
